title: dianthus orientalis subsp. gorganicus
intro: Dianthus orientalis subsp. gorganicus, also known as Gorgan pink, is a perennial plant species within the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). The plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Consuming Dianthus orientalis subsp. gorganicus can provide several health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
